behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural MT output quality (grammar + meaning preservation), which a stub cannot validate (LLM-DEP). Not multi-step agent pipeline, so Workflow is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run real source sentences through the production translation API and evaluate semantic preservation and grammatical coherence against human-approved references/rubric thresholds.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration (API client -> fallback translator -> response path). A controlled 503 from a test double is sufficient to expose fallback wiring/state bugs (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating a real external HTTP error contract shape or real-network failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service components with a test API client that returns 503; assert caller receives fallback translation (no error) and fallback path is invoked.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real interaction between fallback logic and SQLite persistence (query/schema/data mapping), beyond pure function logic. Rejected System: SQLite is local DB integration, no explicit external contract/round-trip boundary in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a temporary SQLite DB with known phrase->translation rows; execute fallback translator and assert exact stored translation is returned for matching phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Needs algorithm + persistence path validation together (compute + store). Unit tests cover distance math, but integration catches serialization/schema/write-path issues. Rejected Agentic: correctness here is deterministic edit-distance computation, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute comparison pipeline with fixed primary/fallback strings, then read persisted metric from storage and verify expected edit-distance value and record linkage.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is concurrent orchestration/side-effect wiring (primary selection + background metric logging) across local modules. Rejected Agentic: behavior text checks routing/preference and background logging, not whether model output quality is correct.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic primary and fallback providers enabled, assert response uses primary output immediately and verify background job persisted comparison metric after completion.
